config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. I do not believe society’s interests should automatically take priority over individual rights, because that opens the door to abuse by governments or majorities. In my view, a healthy society protects basic rights first, then balances them with public needs only when there is a clear and limited reason. From my perspective, the individual should not be treated as disposable for the sake of a vague idea of the common good.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus selalu didahulukan atas hak individu, karena di Amerika saya melihat betapa mudahnya alasan “demi kebaikan bersama” dipakai untuk membenarkan penyalahgunaan kekuasaan. Hak individu bukan penghalang bagi masyarakat; justru itu batas yang mencegah mayoritas atau pemerintah menginjak minoritas. Tentu ada keadaan tertentu seperti kesehatan publik atau keamanan, tetapi pengecualian itu harus sempit, jelas, dan bisa dipertanggungjawabkan, bukan dijadikan alasan umum. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang sehat bukan yang mengorbankan orang per orang, melainkan yang melindungi martabat tiap individu sambil menyeimbangkan kepentingan bersama secara hati-hati.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society should automatically come before the individual, because in Indonesia we have seen how “for the public good” can be used to silence dissent and excuse unfair treatment. At the same time, I do not see individual rights as absolute in every case, because public health, disaster response, and social order do require some limits that are narrow and accountable. What matters is not giving society blanket priority, but proving that any restriction on rights is necessary, proportionate, and temporary. If the common good is used as a slogan to overpower ordinary people, then it is not a healthy society at all.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak pernyataan itu, karena dari sudut pandang saya di Amerika, menjadikan kepentingan masyarakat sebagai prioritas umum di atas hak individu terlalu mudah berubah menjadi pembenaran bagi negara atau mayoritas untuk melampaui batas. Poin tentang pembatasan yang sempit, proporsional, dan sementara memang penting, tetapi itu justru menunjukkan bahwa hak individu harus jadi titik awal, bukan sesuatu yang bisa disisihkan kapan saja demi “kebaikan bersama.” Bagi saya, masyarakat yang kuat bukan yang meminta orang mengalah terus-menerus, melainkan yang membangun kepercayaan dengan melindungi kebebasan dasar semua orang, termasuk minoritas yang paling rentan.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not support giving society blanket priority over the individual, because in Indonesia that kind of language is often used to justify silencing criticism and protecting power, not serving people. At the same time, rights cannot be treated as unlimited in every situation, since public health, disaster response, and basic order sometimes require temporary limits that are clearly justified. The real standard should be whether a restriction is necessary, proportionate, and accountable, not whether it sounds good as “for the common good.” If society routinely overrides individuals, then it stops being a healthy society and becomes a system that trains people to accept abuse.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak pernyataan itu: kepentingan masyarakat tidak boleh otomatis berada di atas hak individu. Dari pengalaman saya di Amerika, alasan “demi kebaikan bersama” terlalu sering dipakai untuk membenarkan kekuasaan yang berlebihan, dan justru minoritas yang paling mudah dirugikan. Memang ada situasi seperti kesehatan publik atau keadaan darurat yang menuntut pembatasan, tetapi itu harus benar-benar sempit, sementara, dan bisa dipertanggungjawabkan. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang kuat bukan yang terus meminta individu mengalah, melainkan yang melindungi kebebasan dasar sebagai fondasi kepentingan bersama.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
